footer.tagline: Freelance marketplace for local and remote work.

hero.title: Hire freelancers faster - nearby or remote
hero.subtitle: Search by skill and city, review real profiles, and hire without scattered chats.
hero.supportingLine: All bids and conversations stay tied to the job.
hero.locationNote: Use city when you need a local freelancer, or leave it empty for remote freelancer results.
hero.howStep1: Post a job or browse freelancers.
hero.howStep3: Chat and hire in one place.
hero.howFootnote: No sign-in required to search—create an account when you are ready to post or send proposals.
hero.searchSubtitle: Real listings · updates with the community
hero.keywordPlaceholder: What do you need? e.g. photographer, video editor
hero.cityHint: Add a city to bias toward nearby profiles; leave it empty for broader remote results.

categories.title: Hire freelancers by category
categories.subtitle: Start from a lane, then narrow by city or go fully remote.
categories.secondaryCta: Need active briefs? Open job board ->

useCases.title: How teams use NearWork
useCases.subtitle: Use nearby for local work, remote for async work, or hybrid when both matter.

onsite.title: Event photographer in your city
onsite.body: Use city and nearby signals to shortlist freelancers who can show up.

remote.title: Video editor for weekly content
remote.body: Compare bids on turnaround, revisions, and scope before hiring.

hybrid.title: Tutor with local and online sessions
hybrid.body: Set location and schedule expectations from the first brief.

finalCta.title: Browse first, then post when you are ready to hire freelancers
finalCta.subtitle: NearWork keeps local and remote hiring in one practical workflow.

jobs.pageDescription: Active client briefs—filter by category, place, and work mode, then open a row for the full scope.
jobs.locationHint: Matches when the job lists a city.
jobs.resultHint: Title + budget at a glance—open for the full brief.
jobs.sidebarBody: Post a brief with budget and location so the right people can send proposals.
jobs.emptyCategoryTitle: No jobs yet in this category
jobs.emptyCategoryBody: Nothing open matches that category right now. The board only shows what clients have actually posted.
jobs.emptyCategoryStep1: Clear category and scan the full board.
jobs.emptyCategoryStep2: Loosen keyword or city if you stacked filters.
jobs.emptyCategoryStep3: Post a brief if you are the one hiring.
jobs.emptyFiltersBody: Early access boards stay sparse—filters are doing their job even when the answer is zero.
jobs.emptyFiltersStep1: Reset work mode to Any, then narrow again.
jobs.emptyFiltersStep2: Drop city if the role might be remote.
jobs.emptyFiltersStep3: Use one keyword from the job title you remember.
jobs.emptyDefaultTitle: No open jobs on the board
jobs.emptyDefaultBody: The public job list is empty until clients post real briefs—there is no demo data behind this view.
jobs.emptyDefaultStep1: Clients: post the first brief with budget and work mode.
jobs.emptyDefaultStep2: Freelancers: browse the directory and polish your profile while jobs ramp up.
jobs.emptyDefaultStep3: Everyone: early access means the feed will move—bookmark and check back.

freelancers.pageDescription: Live profiles—filter by skill, city, and work mode. Turn on nearby when place matters; go remote when it does not.
freelancers.categoryHint: Matches anyone with at least one skill in that category.
freelancers.cityHint: NearWork treats city as a first-class signal for on-site and hybrid work—add yours to discover nearby freelancers.
freelancers.nearbyIdleHint: Optional: allow browser location to prioritize freelancers close to you. City search remains available.
freelancers.nearbyErrorUnsupported: Geolocation is not supported in this browser.
freelancers.nearbyErrorDenied: Location permission denied. You can still search by city.
freelancers.nearbyErrorLookup: Could not determine your location. Try again or use city search.
freelancers.nearbyBannerBody: Within {{radius}} km · closest first. Distance on cards when coordinates exist.
freelancers.cityMatchBody: Profiles mentioning “{{city}}”—pair with work mode to separate remote vs on-site.
freelancers.resultHint: Open a card for the full profile and reviews.
freelancers.emptyCategoryTitle: No freelancers in this category yet
freelancers.emptyCategoryBody: Nobody on the directory lists skills under that category right now—that is a real empty state, not a bug.
freelancers.emptyCategoryStep1: Clear category and try a broader keyword.
freelancers.emptyCategoryStep2: Switch work mode to Any if you over-filtered remote vs on-site.
freelancers.emptyCategoryStep3: Check open jobs if you are hiring instead of browsing talent.
freelancers.emptyFiltersBody: The directory is still growing in early access—tight filters often return zero even when people are signing up.
freelancers.emptyFiltersStep1: Remove city or widen radius if you used nearby.
freelancers.emptyFiltersStep2: Try remote-only for roles that do not need someone on-site.
freelancers.emptyFiltersStep3: Shorten the keyword to one strong term.
freelancers.emptyDefaultBody: When freelancers publish profiles with city and work mode, they show up here automatically—nothing to seed by hand.
freelancers.emptyDefaultStep1: If you are a freelancer, complete your profile and set availability.
freelancers.emptyDefaultStep2: If you are hiring, post a job so proposals can start.
freelancers.emptyDefaultStep3: Come back after early-access invites roll out—this list will move first.

jobDetail.ownerHint: Shortlist to compare later-hire when you are confident every scope detail is clear.
jobDetail.freelancerHint: Send a proposal with price and timing-clients compare you in context with the brief.
jobDetail.ownerAcceptedDesc: Hiring decision is complete. Continue with contract and coordination for delivery.
jobDetail.ownerCompareDesc: Compare freelancers by price, profile readiness, and location signals. Start with pending decisions.
jobDetail.freelancerActionDesc: Sending a proposal requires a signed-in freelancer account. Browse this page freely; sign in or register when you are ready.
jobDetail.pendingDecisionHint: Shortlist to compare, accept when ready
jobDetail.noProposalsYet: No proposals yet. Keep this job open or refine the brief so freelancers can respond with confidence.
jobDetail.bidHintShortlisted: Ready to hire when you're confident
jobDetail.signInToSend: Sign in to send a proposal
jobDetail.originalLanguageOnly: Translated copy is not available yet; showing original text.

forbidden.title: Access denied
forbidden.description: You do not have permission to open this area of the app. Use the dashboard for your account type, or sign in with a different user.

nearbySearch.title: Nearby search
nearbySearch.description: Combine geolocation inputs with your GeoService and search APIs.
nearbySearch.mapDescription: Map UI reads query state from URL or form - logic stays in hooks/services.
nearbySearch.resultsDescription: Render freelancers or jobs returned from the search layer.

earlyAccess.description: We are shipping in public: real jobs, real freelancers, and honest limits—while we finish payments and edge cases.
earlyAccess.whyBody: A marketplace only works if briefs and bids feel trustworthy. We would rather grow with a small, vocal community than fake scale.
earlyAccess.todayItem1: Browse open jobs and freelancer profiles
earlyAccess.todayItem2: Post jobs and collect bids (as a client)
earlyAccess.todayItem3: Lean on city and work mode when nearby work matters
earlyAccess.evolvingPrefix: Payouts, dispute tooling, and some polish paths are not finished. If something breaks, use

pricing.descriptionSuffix: We are not selling you a plan today—we want real jobs, real profiles, and feedback while the product settles. Here is how money works right now (and what might change later).
pricing.currentlyFreeBody1: Using NearWork during early access does not require a paid subscription. You can browse public jobs and freelancer profiles, create an account, and use the core flows we have shipped without hitting a pricing gate.
pricing.currentlyFreeBold: No subscription is required right now.
pricing.currentlyFreeBody2: If we ever ask for payment for platform access, we will say so clearly here and in-product first—not through hidden charges on work you already started.
pricing.donationBody: If you want to help cover costs while we build, you may see an optional way to donate inside the product. It is entirely voluntary: skipping it does not limit your profile, your jobs, or how you show up in search.
pricing.futureBody1: We may introduce paid options down the road—things like boosted visibility, team features, or other add-ons. Nothing is finalized; we will not publish fake price tags or countdowns to pressure you.
pricing.futureBody2: When paid tiers exist, this page will list what you get and what it costs before we turn anything on for new charges.
pricing.ctaBody: You do not need a price sheet to try NearWork—look around, then sign up if it fits.

help.description: NearWork is a small product in early access. We are not a 24/7 support desk yet—this page is a honest anchor for how to reach us and what we can help with.
help.contactBody: For questions, bugs, or trust and safety reports, email us at the address below. We read everything; response time depends on volume while the team is small.
help.contactNote: Placeholder—replace with your real support inbox before you point customers here.
help.topicsBody: Full articles can live here later; for now these are the buckets we expect to document.
help.topic1Body: Creating an account, roles (client vs freelancer), and what you can do in early access.
help.topic2Body: Posting a brief, reviewing proposals, and what happens after you pick someone.
help.topic3Body: City, work mode, and how you show up to clients searching nearby or remote.
help.topic4Body: Placeholder topic—full guidance will land when checkout and payouts are wired end to end.
help.topic5Body: Errors, abuse, or scam patterns—reach out with what you saw and when; screenshots help.
help.selfServeItem2Suffix: what works today vs what is still moving
help.selfServeItem3Suffix: free during early access; donations optional

how.description: NearWork connects clients and freelancers around briefs, proposals, and delivery—with your city and work mode in the loop when place matters. The product is real; the roadmap is still open.
how.clientsSubtitle: When you need talent for a defined piece of work.
how.clientsStep1Body: Describe the outcome, budget, and timeline. Say whether you need someone nearby, remote, or hybrid—clients use location fields when the work is not only online.
how.clientsStep2Body: Freelancers respond with proposals on your job. You compare scope, price, and timing in one place instead of scattered threads.
how.clientsStep3Body: Choose who fits, align on milestones, and keep decisions next to the work. The details depend on your project—NearWork is the shared starting line, not a replacement for how you run delivery day to day.
how.freelancersSubtitle: When you want briefs that respect how and where you work.
how.freelancersStep1Body: Add skills, work mode, and where you can show up in person. That helps clients who care about nearby talent actually discover you—not just your headline.
how.freelancersStep2Body: Use the public job board and filters (including city and mode) to find briefs that match how you work. Listings are live data from the marketplace—not a demo feed.
how.freelancersStep3Body: Reply with clear scope, price, and timing. A strong proposal is specific to the brief—generic pitches do not help you or the client.
how.nearbyBody: Nearby fits shoots, installs, on-site support, and anything that needs someone in a place. Remote fits work that ships online. Hybrid sits in between. You pick the mode per job or per profile so expectations stay explicit.
how.earlyAccessBody: NearWork is still in early access: features and coverage will grow, and some flows will change as we learn from real usage. We are not pretending the marketplace is complete—we are inviting people who are okay building with us.
how.earlyAccessLink: Read what early access means today →
how.ctaBody: Create an account to post or bid, or explore the public board first—no hype, just the same product everyone else sees.

terms.title: Terms of service
terms.subtitle: Draft placeholder for early access. Replace with counsel-reviewed terms before charging money at scale.
terms.body1: By using NearWork during early access you agree to use the product responsibly: no fraudulent listings, no harassment, and no attempts to bypass platform safety controls we add over time.
terms.body2: Payments and dispute resolution may rely on third-party providers; final legal language will describe those relationships clearly.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.subtitle: Draft placeholder for early access. Replace with counsel-reviewed text before production marketing.
privacy.body1: NearWork processes account data (email, name, role) and content you post (jobs, bids, messages) to run the marketplace. We use cookies for signed-in sessions. We do not sell personal data to third parties in this early-access build.
